_global.text:
## Summary Build a minimal single-container FastAPI + SQLite web app that serves a static HTML/JS landing page titled "FaithfulD Book Notes" with create / list-newest-first / delete CRUD for book notes. ## Scope ### Files to modify - None substantive (greenfield). `README.md` optionally updated with run instructions. ### Files to create - `backend/app/main.py` â€” FastAPI app: mounts static frontend, defines API routes, health endpoints, DB init on startup. - `backend/app/db.py` â€” SQLite connection helper + schema bootstrap (`notes` table). - `backend/app/models.py` â€” Pydantic request/response schemas (`NoteCreate`, `NoteOut`). - `backend/requirements.txt` â€” `fastapi`, `uvicorn[standard]`, `pydantic`. - `backend/static/index.html` â€” landing page with `

FaithfulD Book Notes

`, add-note form, notes list container. - `backend/static/app.js` â€” fetches/renders notes newest-first, handles add + delete via API, reads `?confirmDelete=` for delete-confirm state. - `backend/static/styles.css` â€” minimal styling. - `Dockerfile` â€” python:3.12-slim base, install deps, copy `backend/`, run uvicorn on `0.0.0.0:8080`. - `.dockerignore` â€” exclude `.git`, `__pycache__`, local `*.db`. ### Dependencies - FastAPI, Uvicorn (ASGI server), Pydantic. Python stdlib `sqlite3` (no ORM). No frontend framework â€” vanilla HTML/JS. ### Integrations - None (no third-party APIs or SDKs). ### Assumptions - **Stack choice:** FastAPI + SQLite + vanilla static frontend, single container. Chosen for minimal footprint, no build step, and clean Docker deploy via Colossus. No stack was pre-declared. - **No auth:** Spec lists "No auth, no user accounts" as an explicit non-goal; the auth baseline is overridden by the authoritative spec. No login, guards, or admin. - **Persistence path:** SQLite DB at `/data/faithfuld.db` (env `DB_PATH`, default `./faithfuld.db` locally) so it can map to a persistent volume; startup creates the dir/table if missing. - **Port:** App listens on `8080` (common Colossus convention); adjustable via `PORT` env. - **Serving model:** FastAPI serves both the JSON API (`/api/*`) and the static SPA from the same origin â€” no CORS needed. - **Note fields:** `id` (int PK), `title` (text, required, non-empty), `body` (text, required, short), `created_at` (ISO timestamp). Newest-first = `ORDER BY id DESC`. - **Routing:** Single page; the only non-trivial UI state (delete confirmation) is URL-addressable via `?confirmDelete=` restored on load. Transient form input is exempt. ## Implementation Plan ### Step 1: Database layer **File:** `backend/app/db.py` **Change:** `get_conn()` returning a `sqlite3.Connection` (row factory = `sqlite3.Row`). `init_db()` reads `DB_PATH`, ensures parent dir exists, and runs `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notes (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, title TEXT NOT NULL, body TEXT NOT NULL, created_at TEXT NOT NULL)`. ### Step 2: Schemas **File:** `backend/app/models.py` **Change:** `NoteCreate(title: str, body: str)` with validators enforcing non-empty, trimmed, reasonable max lengths (title â‰¤200, body â‰¤1000). `NoteOut(id, title, body, created_at)`. ### Step 3: API + app wiring **File:** `backend/app/main.py` **Change:** Create `FastAPI()` app. On startup call `init_db()`. Routes: - `GET /api/health` â†’ `{"status":"ok"}`. - `GET /api/health/deep` â†’ runs `SELECT 1` against DB, returns ok/500. - `GET /api/notes` â†’ all notes `ORDER BY id DESC` â†’ `list[NoteOut]`. - `POST /api/notes` â†’ insert `NoteCreate`, set `created_at` = UTC ISO now, return created `NoteOut` (201). - `DELETE /api/notes/{note_id}` â†’ delete row; 204 on success, 404 if absent. Mount static: `app.mount("/", StaticFiles(directory="backend/static", html=True), name="static")` (mounted last so `/api/*` wins). ### Step 4: Landing page **File:** `backend/static/index.html` **Change:** `

FaithfulD Book Notes

` (ensures literal "FaithfulD" renders). Form with `#title` input, `#body` textarea, submit button. `
    ` list container. Links `styles.css` and `app.js`. ### Step 5: Frontend logic **File:** `backend/static/app.js` **Change:** On load: `loadNotes()` â†’ `GET /api/notes`, render each as list item with title, body, timestamp, and a Delete button. Form submit â†’ `POST /api/notes` then reload list and clear form. Delete button sets `?confirmDelete=`; on load if that param is present, show inline confirm â†’ on confirm `DELETE /api/notes/{id}`, then clear param and reload. Escape user text via `textContent` to avoid injection. ### Step 6: Styling **File:** `backend/static/styles.css` **Change:** Minimal readable layout â€” centered container, form spacing, note cards. ### Step 7: Containerization **File:** `Dockerfile` **Change:** `FROM python:3.12-slim`; set workdir; copy `requirements.txt` and `pip install`; copy `backend/`; `ENV DB_PATH=/data/faithfuld.db PORT=8080`; `EXPOSE 8080`; `CMD ["sh","-c","uvicorn backend.app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port ${PORT}"]`. Add `.dockerignore`. ## Testing Strategy - **Health:** `curl /api/health` â†’ 200 ok; `/api/health/deep` â†’ 200 with DB reachable. - **Create:** `POST /api/notes` with title+body â†’ 201 and returned object has id + created_at. - **List order:** create two notes; `GET /api/notes` returns the second one first (id DESC). - **Persist:** restart container (same volume) â†’ notes still returned. - **Delete:** `DELETE /api/notes/{id}` â†’ 204; subsequent GET omits it; deleting unknown id â†’ 404. - **UI:** load page, confirm "FaithfulD Book Notes" renders in `

    `; add a note via form â†’ appears at top; delete via button + confirm â†’ disappears. - **Validation:** empty title or body â†’ 422. ## Risks - Container filesystem is ephemeral â€” DB must sit on a mounted/persistent path (`/data`) or notes are lost on redeploy; documented via `DB_PATH`. - SQLite single-writer is fine for this low-traffic, no-concurrency-critical app; not suited to horizontal scaling (out of scope). - Static mount ordering must keep `/api/*` routes resolving before the catch-all static mount.
